---
title: "Choosing Evaluation Methods"
description: "Study note on Choosing Evaluation Methods in Magenta Book."
book_id: "magenta-book"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Major section"
source_pages: "6"
citation_key: "hm_treasury_evaluation_task_force_2020_magenta_book"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:magenta-book:magenta-book-3-evaluation-methods:c0002"
source_status: "needs_review"
---
## Source

<div class="chapter-meta"><span>2020</span><span>MCP evaluation-texts</span><span>Status: needs_review</span><span>Source pages 6</span><span>Major section</span></div>
This note summarises the MCP research-library section "Choosing Evaluation Methods" from HM Treasury and Evaluation Task Force's *Magenta Book: Central Government guidance on evaluation* [@hm_treasury_evaluation_task_force_2020_magenta_book]. It is a paraphrased study note based on source-linked retrieval records, not a substitute for the source text.

## Core Argument

The methods chapter stresses that method choice requires iteration as scope, timing, resources, and questions become clearer.

## Study Summary

The methods chapter stresses that method choice requires iteration as scope, timing, resources, and questions become clearer.

Different methods answer different questions and have different resource implications.

Stakeholder involvement matters because method choice affects what can credibly be answered.

## Key Concepts

- Method selection
- Theory-based methods
- Experimental methods
- Evidence synthesis

## Source Location

- Section path: 3. Evaluation methods
- Evidence records: `chunk:magenta-book:magenta-book-3-evaluation-methods:c0002`

## Practical Implications

- Revisit evaluation questions when methods prove infeasible.
- Communicate any deprioritizing of questions clearly.
- Use stakeholders' context knowledge when judging method assumptions.

## Connections

- Book overview: [Overview](index.qmd)
- Concepts: [Core Concepts](concepts.qmd)
- Map: [Chapter Map](chapter-map.qmd)
- Practice synthesis: [Practice Implications](practice-implications.qmd)

## Study Prompts

1. What is the main evaluation problem that Choosing Evaluation Methods helps diagnose?
2. Which assumptions would need evidence before using this guidance in a live evaluation?
3. How would this change the way an evaluator scopes questions, methods, or reporting?
4. Where could the guidance be misused if treated as a fixed template?

## References
